description: You are a university astronomy professor preparing a lecture on Earth's axial precession. Demonstrate that Thuban (Alpha Draconis) was the pole star during the age of the Egyptian pyramids (~2560 BCE) by simulating the night sky from the Great Pyramid of Giza (29.9792° N, 31.1342° E, 60 m) on June 21, 2560 BCE (Year -2559) at 22:00 UTC. Configure the view with atmosphere and ground disabled, and the equatorial coordinate grid and constellation lines enabled. Center the view on Thuban and save a screenshot to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/. Document the lecture notes in /home/ga/Desktop/precession_notes.txt, ensuring they include the terms 'Thuban', 'precession', and '2560 BCE'.
